steps.title: Identify the contrast the blank must complete
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two outcomes of one-way trips: some locations end up with an excess of bicycles while "other areas" face "insufficient supply." The blank describes the excess-side condition — it must capture a location that is overfull or oversupplied with bicycles, forming a clear contrast with shortage.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — susceptible to: Means sensitive to or easily affected by. Locations are not sensitive to bicycles; they accumulate them. Wrong part of speech semantically — the word requires a harm or influence, not a commodity. ✗
  • Choice B — contingent on: Means dependent on. No dependency relationship exists between a location and the bicycles stacking up there. ✗
  • Choice C — saturated with: Means filled to capacity or overstocked. Directly captures the excess-bicycle condition and contrasts cleanly with "insufficient supply." ✓
  • Choice D — depleted of: Means emptied of. This would make both sides of the contrast describe shortage — eliminating any contrast at all. ✗
That is the correct answer.
